zotero.abstractNote: Balanced steady-state free precession (bSSFP) is a fast magnetic resonance imaging sequence that is highly sensitive to diffusion. However, the bSSFP signal is also sensitive to off-resonance effects, which can lead to signal voids and artifacts. In this work, we propose a neural network-driven approach to map multiple diffusion metrics from bSSFP data, leveraging the asymmetries in the bSSFP frequency profile. We demonstrate that our method can produce high-resolution diffusion maps in a fraction of the time required by conventional methods.
